headline: UK offshore CO2 transport & storage decommissioning-funding regime takes effect
summary: The Carbon Dioxide Transport and Storage (Financing of Costs of Offshore Decommissioning) Regulations 2026 (SI 2026/632) come into force on 10 July 2026, implementing s.92 of the Energy Act 2023. Licensed CO2 T&S companies must ring-fence a proportion of user fees in an offshore decommissioning fund over an ~25-year operating life.
co2crc_note: Offshore CO2 T&S decommissioning-liability funding precedent directly relevant to Australia's OPGGS Act offshore storage regime.

headline: Qatar-based voluntary standard integrates an industry-led CCS crediting framework
summary: Carbon Pulse's daily ticker reports a Qatar-based voluntary carbon standard has become the latest crediting body to fold an industry-developed CCS methodology into its programme.
co2crc_note: Convergence of CCS with voluntary-carbon-market rulebooks; a Middle-East signal, limited direct CO2CRC relevance.
extractor_note: Radar item (published 8 Jul, just outside daily window).

headline: US opinion: CCS lacks a regulatory framework and lets corporations profit
summary: A Prism Reports commentary argues US sub-seabed/saline CO2 injection is advancing ahead of a settled regulatory regime and frames CCS as privatising profit while socialising long-term risk.
co2crc_note: Social-licence critique echoing recent Australian commentary on CCS liability and public risk.
extractor_note: Radar item (published 8 Jul, just outside daily window).
